title: fragaria virginiana var. ovalis
intro: Fragaria virginiana var. ovalis, also known as the oval-leaved wood strawberry or oval-leaf woodland strawberry, is a variety of wild strawberry native to eastern North America. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic qualities. The oval-leaved wood strawberry has been reported to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of conditions, from minor wounds to digestive issues.
